commit.message:
Fix npm detection: use exit code instead of output string

The previous logic checked if "npm" appeared in output, but error messages
like "which: npm: Command not found" also contain "npm", causing false
positives. Now uses the exit code from `which` directly.

commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

commit.message:
Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Phase XV: Information & Dynamics (T186-T200) — 15 new theorems, 77 tests

15 new theorems spanning random walk dynamics, effective resistance,
spectral zeta functions, Rényi entropy, and walk-matrix algebra:

- T186: Kemeny constant K = 801/20
- T187: Kirchhoff index Kf = 267/2
- T188: Effective resistance R_adj = Phi3/(2v), R_non = Phi6/v
- T189: Return probability P4 = 13/432
- T190: Spectral gap mixing: dimO/k = 2/3, q/lam = 3/2
- T191: Estrada index from SRG eigenvalues
- T192: Spectral zeta zeta(1) = q^2*Phi6/mu
- T193: Transition eigenvalues 1, 1/6, -1/3
- T194: Vertex stabilizer |GL(2,3)|*27 = 1296
- T195: Complement determinant det(A_bar) = 3^42
- T196: Eigenspace projections
- T197: Resolvent spectrum R(theta) = 25/7
- T198: Walk matrix (A^3)_adj = dim(F4) = 52
- T199: Rényi entropy H2 = log2(30)
- T200: Wiener index W = 1320

Total: 200 theorems, 1220+ tests across 14 test suites.
Website updated with Phase XV table and all counts.

commit.message:
fix: comprehensive health check — 40+ issues across backend, frontend, devops

Critical:
- tag.py: remove await from sync session.delete()
- external.py: pass UUID object (not str) to DownloadJob
- import_router.py: path traversal guard with is_relative_to()
- login/page.tsx: use router.replace + mountedRef to prevent race condition
- Reader/hooks.ts: clean up Image prefetch objects to prevent memory leak
- backup.sh/restore.sh: use docker compose exec, stop all services on restore

High:
- auth.py: safer parameterized UPDATE, atomic avatar write
- ws.py: fix duplicate WebSocketDisconnect handler
- worker.py: wrap _sha256() with asyncio.to_thread()
- export.py: add 2GB ZIP size limit
- models.py: add FK on api_tokens.user_id → users.id
- Reader/index.tsx: webtoon tap zone → <button> for a11y
- ws.ts: dev-mode console.warn on WebSocket error
- useAuth.ts: try/catch on logout with toast
- api.ts: 401 handling for avatar upload
- SWR: add dedupingInterval/focusThrottleInterval
- docker-compose: health check timeout, stop_grace_period
- nginx: X-Forwarded-Proto on all locations, /api/download/ rate limit

Medium:
- download.py: transaction wrapping for DB+ARQ enqueue
- import_router.py: support .webp/.avif/.heic media types
- types.ts: tighten DownloadJob.progress type
- Reader: clean stale IntersectionObserver refs
- library/page.tsx: clear debounce timer on unmount

Other:
- Fix PWA Dockerfile: add HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0 for standalone mode
- Fix health check: use 127.0.0.1 instead of localhost (Alpine IPv6)
- Regenerate all icons: remove baked-in checkerboard, transparent favicons
- middleware.ts: exclude icon/favicon from auth redirect
- Fix test fixtures for updated API signatures

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Next.js standalone server.js expects .next/static and public/
to be in .next/standalone/ directory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase 7: Snowflake credential caching, SQL generation command, Jinja2 template cleanup

Enable Snowflake built-in credential caching (client_store_temporary_credential,
client_request_mfa_token) by default to avoid repeated SSO/MFA prompts.

Add `qdo sql` command group with 7 subcommands: select, insert, ddl, task, udf,
procedure, scratch. All use Jinja2 templates for SQL structure and get_columns()
metadata. Scratch fetches sample rows and formats values in Python.

Add trim_blocks/lstrip_blocks to Jinja2 Environment and fix profile templates
for compatibility.

commit.message:
Rework JB finalization: drop dropbear, auto-bootstrap on first boot (#141)

* fix: build

* fix: remove [trusted=yes] from Havoc apt source

The inline [trusted=yes] option can cause issues with Sileo's
source parser. The apt-get calls already use AllowUnauthenticated
flags, making it redundant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: main actor crash in VPhoneControl + IPA extraction failures

VPhoneControl: pending request handlers are @MainActor-isolated closures
but were called from DispatchQueue.global() in the read loop and timeout
handler, causing dispatch_assert_queue_fail crashes. Wrap all
pending.handler() calls in DispatchQueue.main.async.

unarchive: the recent ARCHIVE_EXTRACT_SECURE_* hardening (ef02d50) broke
IPA extraction on iOS because:
- SECURE_NOABSOLUTEPATHS: we set absolute output paths on entries
- SECURE_SYMLINKS: iOS system paths (/var, /tmp) are symlinks
- archive_write_header failures were silently swallowed due to if/else if
  structure, making extraction report success with no files extracted

Fix by keeping only SECURE_NODOTDOT, resolving symlinks in extraction
path, fixing header error handling, removing unnecessary ACL/FFLAGS
flags, and surfacing libarchive errors in the install response.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* remove dropbear SSH daemon from guest

Drop all dropbear setup: LaunchDaemon plist injection, host key
generation, daemon deployment, and SSH availability messages.
Guest communication is handled by vphoned over vsock.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: liblaunch compat stub + automatic JB first-boot setup

liblaunch_compat.dylib: stub exporting _launch_active_user_switch
(missing from PCC VM's libSystem.B.dylib) so procursus binaries
like launchctl can load. Deployed to /cores/, loaded via
DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES in LaunchDaemon environment and JB profile.

vphone_jb_setup.sh: first-boot script replacing the SSH-based
cfw_install_jb_post.sh. Runs as a LaunchDaemon on first normal
boot and performs all JB finalization: /var/jb symlink,
prep_bootstrap, markers, Sileo, apt setup, TrollStore Lite.
Idempotent with done marker. Logs to /var/log/vphone_jb_setup.log.

Removes the cfw_install_jb_finalize make target and the entire
SSH/iproxy/sshpass-based post-boot flow from setup_machine.sh.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update AGENTS.md firmware table, gitignore build artifacts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: launchctl wrapper uses absolute path + timeout to prevent hangs

- Use absolute path to launchctl.real instead of relative dirname,
  fixing "not found" when called via /var/jb/bin/launchctl symlink
- Add 5s timeout so launchctl doesn't hang when launchd is
  unresponsive on PCC VMs — always exits 0 for dpkg postinst compat
- Symlink /var/jb/bin/launchctl -> /var/jb/usr/bin/launchctl so both
  paths work (openssh postinst uses the /bin/ path)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: replace liblaunch_compat dylib stub with iosbinpack64 launchctl symlink

Procursus launchctl crashes on PCC VMs due to missing
_launch_active_user_switch symbol. Rather than a custom dylib stub,
simply symlink iosbinpack64's launchctl into /var/jb — it talks to
launchd fine and always exits 0, which is all dpkg scripts need.

- Remove liblaunch_compat.c, its build target, signing, and deployment
- Remove DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES from setup script and plist
- Replace launchctl wrapper with symlinks to /iosbinpack64/bin/launchctl
- Both /var/jb/usr/bin/launchctl and /var/jb/bin/launchctl are covered

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Add world-class PDF Editor: full annotation suite, 100% client-side, zero upload

Features:
- Add text with font family, size, color, bold/italic, alignment
- Freehand drawing with pen tool (color, width, opacity)
- Shapes: rectangle, circle, line, arrow (outline/fill, color)
- Highlight tool with semi-transparent overlay
- Whiteout/redact tool for covering sensitive content
- Add images from device (auto-scaled, draggable)
- Signature pad: draw signature and place on PDF
- 8 professional stamps: APPROVED, DRAFT, CONFIDENTIAL, COPY, VOID, FINAL, REVIEWED, URGENT
- Eraser tool to remove annotations
- Select, move, duplicate, delete any annotation
- Full undo/redo history (50 steps)
- Multi-page support with thumbnail navigation
- Zoom in/out/fit controls
- Keyboard shortcuts: V/T/P/R/H + Ctrl+Z/Y/D/S + Delete/Escape
- Properties bar: 10 colors, font picker, line width, opacity slider, fill toggle
- Save PDF: all annotations baked into the file using pdf-lib
- PDF.js rendering via CDN (same pattern as pdf-to-image)
- 100% client-side — files never leave the device
- No watermarks, no limits, no signup
- SEO: targets "PDF editor online" (high volume, competitive)

Technical: PDF.js for rendering, HTML/CSS/Canvas for annotation overlay,
pdf-lib for saving. Hybrid approach: canvas for freehand drawing,
HTML elements for text/shapes/images (better interactivity).

fix verifier407A geometric check and disable idle DB connections

- Replace string-comparison verifier with geometric validation for 407A
- Disable connection pool idle reuse in eval.go to prevent read-only transaction errors from poisoned connections

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(node): prevent fork sync reorg to shorter/lighter chains

Add two guards to execute_fork_sync_reorg that prevent cascading height
loss:

1. Chain length guard: abort if new chain would be shorter than current
2. Weight comparison: compute real weight delta instead of hardcoding 1

Previously, fork sync always reorged (weight_delta=1) even when the
peer's chain was shorter or lighter. This caused a death spiral: rollback
100 blocks, apply 7, drop 93 in height, trigger more fork syncs, repeat
until trapped in the 90% recovery guard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: patch 3 GRPO bugs and add VisionBridge to train_joystick script

Bug fixes in vla_hands/training/trainer.py:

1. Hard crash (GRPO): guard `_value_head.train()` with None check — default
   rl_algorithm="grpo" sets _value_head=None, causing AttributeError on every
   RL training run.

2. Silent wrong gradients: cache and replay VisionBridge skip features during
   GRPO rollout→update. `_RolloutStep` gains a `vision_features` field; features
   are captured from `_vision_hook` at rollout time and passed back to
   `graft.appendage` during `_grpo_update`. Without this, importance weights
   were computed on different inputs than rollout used, pointing gradients in
   the wrong direction.

3. GRPO advantage collapse: add EMA return baseline so zero-std batches
   (all episodes fail identically, common early in training) still produce
   a useful learning signal instead of zero gradient. Also raise default
   `rl_episodes_per_update` from 8 → 16 in both TrainerConfig and
   CurriculumConfig for more stable advantage estimates.

scripts/train_joystick.py:
- Wrap JoystickAppendage in VisionBridge when vision encoder dim is detectable.
  The script was missing the skip connection that the intro notebook correctly
  uses, causing significantly worse sample efficiency.

All 141 existing tests pass.

Reframe project around dynamic geographic load shifting

The core goal is now NATS-powered global inference routing — shifting
load across regions based on capacity, latency, cost, and availability.
The OpenAI-compatible API (HTTP proxy + JS SDK) is the adoption onramp,
not the central value proposition. Updated design doc sections 1-3 and 6
(milestones) and README to reflect this framing.

repository.name: complr

Refactor edge bonuses to per-player-per-machine matchup comparison

Instead of a blanket per-machine bonus (same % boost for all TWC
players), each cost matrix cell now gets its own bonus comparing
that specific TWC player's venue_adjusted_avg against the assigned
opponent's avg on that machine. This means a strong player on a
machine gets a different edge bonus than a weak player on the same
machine, making opponent weight much more nuanced.

feat: Initial release of Verantyx-CLI v0.2.0-alpha

Cross-Native Hierarchical Multi-Agent System for Claude Code

🌟 Features:
- Cross structure 6-axis knowledge representation
- Hierarchical multi-agent control (Master + Sub-agents)
- Cross Routing Layer for intelligent message routing
- Agent number recognition ("2番のエージェント")
- Progress tracking in Cross structures
- Image to Cross conversion (up to 50,000 points)
- User personality recognition and adaptive control

🏗️ Architecture:
- Cross Routing Layer (top-level information controller)
- Master Agent (Agent 0) controls all sub-agents
- Sub-agents with specialized roles (Analyzer, Designer, etc.)
- 6-axis Cross structure for knowledge representation

📦 Components:
- Terminal UI with multi-agent support
- Cross Generator with progress tracking
- Multi-Agent Controller with routing intelligence
- Image to Cross converter (vision module)
- User personality profile system

🎯 Roadmap:
- v0.2.0 (Alpha): Current release
- v0.3.0 (Beta): Agent-to-agent communication, dynamic task allocation
- v0.4.0: Gemini/Codex support, plugin system
- v1.0.0 (Stable): Production-ready

Generated with Claude Code

commit.message:
fix(dev-parity): implement grep_files in DirectWorkerAdapters

## Summary
- Implement actual grep logic in `DirectWorkerAdapters::grep_files` (was
returning `Ok(vec![])`)
- Validates regex, queries DB for candidate files, applies regex
line-by-line, returns `GrepMatch` results
- Matches behavior of `session_file_store.rs` implementation

## Why
Fixes EVE-58. Dev-mode file search was silently broken — grep always
returned empty results, diverging from production behavior.

## Test plan
- [x] 5 parity tests: single-file match, no match, multi-file/line,
regex patterns, invalid regex error
- [x] All 470 unit tests pass
- [x] `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean

commit.message:
Separate check-in from portrait queue; add org-level portrait cadence

Three distinct pet states on dashboard:
- Portrait Queue: pets getting portraits generated today (new portraitQueueDate field)
- Checked In: physically present but not in portrait queue
- All Pets: not checked in

Schema changes:
- dogs.portrait_queue_date (text) — when set to today, pet is in portrait queue
- organizations.portrait_cadence (text) — weekly/biweekly for daycare auto-rotation

Server:
- POST /api/dogs/:id/queue-portrait and dequeue-portrait endpoints
- Portrait scheduler gates on checkedInAt before queuing
- Scheduler uses org.portraitCadence instead of per-dog updatePreference
- Check-out auto-queues occasional daycare dogs for portrait
- Groomers auto-queue on dog creation (same-day workflow)
- Added portraitQueueDate, visitFrequency, stayNights to DOG_ALLOWED_FIELDS

Client:
- Dashboard: 3-section layout (Portrait Queue / Checked In / All Pets)
- Business settings: portrait cadence selector for daycares
- Removed per-dog updatePreference from quick-add form

commit.message:
fix: ensure /shared-packages is owned by node user (#14)

Pre-create the /shared-packages directory with node:node ownership in
both Dockerfiles. Docker named volumes are initialized with root
ownership by default, so when the orchestrator runs
`npm install --prefix /shared-packages` as the node user it hits
EACCES permission errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add interactive architecture diagrams and animated metrics to Questions

Implemented comprehensive visualization system for system design questions with detailed diagrams and metrics:

## New Diagram Components:

1. **FlowingArrow** - Animated SVG arrows showing data flow with pulsing dots
   - Infinite loop animation with path drawing
   - Color-coded to match component types
   - Staggered delays for sequential appearance

2. **ComponentBox** - Animated system component cards
   - Spring physics entrance animations
   - Hover effects with scale and glow
   - Wiggling icons with periodic rotation
   - Color-themed borders and backgrounds

3. **MetricCard** - Animated statistic displays
   - Slide-in from left with fade
   - Numbers pop in with spring animation
   - Hover expansion effect
   - Color-coded left border accent

4. **ArchitectureDiagram** - Full system architecture visualizations
   - Supports multiple question types (url-shortener, twitter)
   - Two-tier layout: edge layer + data layer
   - Flowing animated connections between components
   - Data flow explanation box

5. **MetricsPanel** - Grid of key performance metrics
   - Responsive grid layout
   - Staggered entrance animations (100ms delay each)
   - Live metric cards for QPS, users, storage, ratios

## Enhanced Questions:

- **URL Shortener**: Added full architecture diagram with Client → CDN → LB → API → Cache + DB flow, plus 4 key metrics
- **Twitter/Social Feed**: Added microservices architecture diagram with API Gateway → Services → Data stores, plus 6 metrics including DAU, QPS, and ratios

## Visual Features:

- Components animate in with spring physics
- Icons rotate subtly on loop
- Flowing data connections with animated dots
- Color-coded by component type (blue=client, green=cdn, purple=api, red=db, orange=cache)
- Hover effects on all interactive elements
- Staggered animations create smooth visual flow
- Comprehensive metrics dashboard for scale understanding

All animations integrate seamlessly with existing question expand/collapse animations.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Note XII: Fine-Grid Detection Landscape

Fine-grid radial sweep (step 0.1) at k=12 resolves the detection
landscape into a two-band structure: inner shell (r=1.3–2.2),
aperiodic coherence gap (r=2.3–2.5), outer shell (r=2.6–3.2), and
dead zone (r≥3.3). Site count is necessary but not sufficient;
azimuthal clustering in the gap region breaks ring winding despite
8–10 sites. Graph-effective limit r*_graph≈3.2 (Ω_eff≈1.26π),
not the analytic r*=4.78.

- sovereign-lattice/fine-grid-landscape/index.html (Note XII, sky theme)
- sovereign-lattice/data/flatfield_screened_k12_finegrid.json (36-radius probe)
- Updated Note XI topbar/sidebar with Note XII link
- Updated Note X topbar/sidebar with Note XII link
- Updated root SLH arc and topbar with Note XII

doesn't significantly increase row height.

commit.message:
fix: Preserve user prompt through script-chain-executor to 3-level-flow

Root cause: script-chain-executor.py consumed Claude Code's stdin
({"prompt": "user message"}) but never passed the prompt field to
child scripts. This caused 3-level-flow.py to receive "[NO MESSAGE]"
on every run, making all downstream decisions (task type, model
selection, skill/agent selection) incorrect and creating stale
flag files that deadlocked Write/Edit tools.

Fix: read_claude_stdin() now captures the hook input first, then
builds initial_context with prompt, message, and cwd fields preserved
so all child scripts in the chain can access the user's message.

Also adds script-chain-executor.py to the repo (was only deployed
locally with no source of truth).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
